title: GTM Regulatory Outlook — Americas — Q2 2026
summary: Twenty-four regulatory events converge across the Americas through September, with the United States driving 16 separate initiatives spanning stablecoin AML programs, DeFi enforcement expansion, and fundamental Bank Secrecy Act reform. Canada implements FATF Travel Rule enforcement while Latin America's largest economies—Chile, Colombia, Argentina—shift from legal ambiguity to active VASP licensing regimes. title: GTM Regulatory Outlook — EMEA — Q2 2026
summary: Twenty-one EU markets, the UK, and six Middle Eastern jurisdictions are simultaneously operationalizing comprehensive crypto regulatory frameworks—MiCA Level 2 standards, AMLA's direct supervision authority, and national licensing transitions. The EU alone shows 211 authorized CASPs with 149 non-compliant entities facing enforcement, while France's July 1 PSAN transition deadline and UK's Q3 authorization cutoff create compressed compliance windows. title: GTM Regulatory Outlook — Asia-Pacific — Q2 2026
summary: Nine Asia-Pacific markets are simultaneously moving from compliance planning to active enforcement across Travel Rule, VASP licensing, and institutional custody frameworks. Australia's AUSTRAC is issuing penalties while South Korea opens traditional financial institutions to crypto custody services and Indonesia transfers exchange supervision to banking regulators.
